Question: What does the happy man not do?
Response: He does not do what the wicked advise him to do.

Question: Where does the happy man not stand?
Response: He does not stand in the pathway of sinners.

Question: Where does the happy man not sit?
Response: He does not sit in the seat of scoffers.

Question: In what does the man delight?
Response: He delights in the instruction of Yahweh.

Question: When does the man meditate on the instruction of Yahweh?
Response: He meditates on it day and night.

Question: What is the man like?
Response: He is like a tree set to grow by streams of water.

Question: What will happen to everything the man does?
Response: Whatever he does will prosper.

Question: What are wicked people like?
Response: They are like the chaff that the wind chases away.

Question: What will happen to wicked people in the judgment?
Response: They will not stand in the judgment.

Question: Who knows the way of the righteous?
Response: Yahweh knows the way of the righteous.

Question: What will happen to the way of the wicked?
Response: The way of the wicked will perish.

Question: Who are the rulers conspiring against?
Response: They are conspiring against Yahweh and against his annointed.

Question: What do the rulers want to tear apart and throw off?
Response: They want to tear apart the restraints Yahweh and his chosen king put on them and cast off their rule.

Question: Who sits in the heavens and mocks the nations who rebel?
Response: The Lord mocks them.

Question: Who has the Lord anointed?
Response: The Lord has anointed his king on Zion, his holy mountain.

Question: What decree does Yahweh give to the psalmist?
Response: Yahweh says, “You are my son! Today I have begotten you.”

Question: What will Yahweh do with the nations and most distant regions of the earth?
Response: Yahweh will give them as an inheritance and possession.

Question: What will happen to the nations?
Response: They will be broken and smashed.

Question: How should the kings and rulers respond to Yahweh’s decree?
Response: They should serve Yahweh in fear and rejoice with trembling.

Question: Why should the kings give allegiance to Yahweh’s son?
Response: They should give him allegiance so that he will not be angry and they will not perish.

Question: What happens to all those who take refuge in the son?
Response: They will have a life that is truly good.

Question: What does David say about how many enemies he has?
Response: David says that his so many enemies who have decided to oppose him.

Question: What does David say Yahweh is to him?
Response: David says Yahweh is a shield that protects him and he is the one who encourages.

Question: What does Yahweh do when David calls out to him?
Response: He answers David from his holy hill.

Question: Who does David say he will not fear because of Yahweh’s protection?
Response: David will not be afraid of the many people who stand against him.

Question: What does David say Yahweh does to his enemies?
Response: Yahweh destroys David’s enemies power like he is breaking animals’ teeth.

Question: From whom does salvation come?
Response: Salvation comes from Yahweh.

Question: Who does David ask to answer when he calls?
Response: David prays for the God of his righteousness to answer when he calls.

Question: What does David say the people are doing to his glory?
Response: They are dishonoring him instead of honoring him.

Question: Who does Yahweh set apart for himself?
Response: Yahweh sets apart the faithful for himself.

Question: What does David tell people to do while lying on their beds?
Response: David tells them to meditate in their hearts and be silent.

Question: What kind of sacrifices does David say to offer?
Response: He says to offer sacrifices of righteousness.

Question: In whom does David say to trust?
Response: He says to trust in Yahweh.

Question: What do many people say?
Response: Many people ask who will show them anything good.

Question: What has Yahweh put in David’s heart?
Response: Yahweh has put more joy in his heart than others have in their hearts when their grain and new wine abound.

Question: Who makes David safe and secure when he lies down in peaceful sleep?
Response: Yahweh alone makes David safe and secure.

Question: Who does David ask listen to what he says and understand his murmuring?
Response: David asks Yahweh to listen to what he says and understand his distressed sigh.

Question: What names does David use for the one who listens to the sound of his cry?
Response: David calls him his king and his God.

Question: What does David say he will do each morning?
Response: David says he will prepare for Yahweh and watch.

Question: What does God certainly not do?
Response: He certainly does not approve of evil, and evil people will not be his guests.

Question: Who will not stand in God’s presence?
Response: The arrogant will not stand in God’s presence.

Question: Whom does God hate?
Response: God hates all who do evil things.

Question: What will God do to liars?
Response: God will destroy liars.

Question: Who does Yahweh despise?
Response: Yahweh despises violent and deceitful men.

Question: Why will David come into Yahweh’s house?
Response: David will come into Yahweh’s house because of Yahweh’s great covenant faithfulness.

Question: How will David bow down toward Yahweh’s holy temple?
Response: David will bow down in reverence.

Question: Why does David ask Yahweh to guide him in righteousness?
Response: David asks Yahweh to guide him in righteousness because of those who are watching him.

Question: How does David describe his enemies?
Response: David describes his enemies as untrustworthy, desiring to destroy people, speaking things that are vile like the smell of an open grave, and flattering people to trap and destroy them.

Question: What does David ask God to do to his enemies because of their many transgressions and their rebellion against God?
Response: David asks God to declare his enemies guilty and to drive them out.

Question: Why should those who take refuge in God shout for joy?
Response: They should shout for joy because God defends them.

Question: What will Yahweh do for the righteous?
Response: Yahweh will bless the righteous and surround them with his favor as with a shield.

Question: What is David asking Yahweh not to do?
Response: He asks Yahweh not to rebuke him in his anger or discipline him in his wrath.

Question: What is David asking Yahweh to do because David is frail?
Response: David asks Yahweh to have mercy on him and to heal him.

Question: Why does David say that Yahweh should rescue and save him?
Response: Because of Yahweh’s covenant faithfulness.

Question: What will not be remembered in death?
Response: In death there is no remembrance of Yahweh.

Question: What does David do to his bed because he is weary and groaning?
Response: All night he soaks it with his tears.

Question: What happens to David’s eyes because of his grief and because of all his adversaries?
Response: His vision becomes weak, so that he cannot see well.

Question: Why should those who practice iniquity get away from David?
Response: Because Yahweh has heard the sound of his weeping and his appeal for mercy and accepts his prayer.

Question: What does David ask God to make happen to his enemies?
Response: He asks that all his enemies will be ashamed and greatly troubled and they will turn back.

Question: In whom does David take refuge?
Response: David takes refuge in Yahweh his God.

Question: What is David asking Yahweh to do?
Response: David is asking Yahweh to save him from those who chase him, and to rescue him.

Question: What does David say should happen to him if he has done wrong?
Response: David says that if he has done wrong, his enemy should be allowed to chase and capture him and leave him lying dead.

Question: What is David asking Yahweh to do in Yahweh’s anger?
Response: David is asking Yahweh show that he is greater than what David's furious enemies can do.

Question: What does David say Yahweh has already ordered?
Response: David says that Yahweh has already ordered judgment.

Question: What does David want Yahweh to do concerning the peoples?
Response: David wants Yahweh to take his rightful place ruling over all the peoples.

Question: Who does David want Yahweh to judge?
Response: David wants Yahweh to judge all the peoples, including himself.

Question: What does David say the righteous God does?
Response: David says God examines thoughts and desires.

Question: Where does David’s protection come from?
Response: David’s protection comes from God.

Question: Who does God save?
Response: God saves the upright in heart.

Question: How does David describe God?
Response: David describes God as a righteous judge who is indignant each day.

Question: What will God do if a person does not repent?
Response: God will sharpen his sword and will prepare his bow for battle.

Question: How does David describe the wicked person who does not repent?
Response: David describes the wicked person as filled with evil intentions, as though pregnant with them, and as one who lies as naturally as giving birth.

Question: What happens to the wicked man who digs a pit?
Response: He falls into the hole he has made.

Question: What happens to the trouble and violence of the wicked man?
Response: He experiences the bad and violent things that he wants to do to others.

Question: For what will David give thanks to Yahweh?
Response: David will give thanks to Yahweh for his righteousness.

Question: To whom will David sing praise?
Response: David will sing praise to the name of Yahweh, the Most High.

Question: Whose name is magnificent in all the earth?
Response: The name of Yahweh our Lord is magnificent.

Question: What did Yahweh establish out of the mouth of children and nursing ones?
Response: Yahweh established strength.

Question: Why did Yahweh establish strength from the mouths of children and nursing ones?
Response: He established it in order to cause the enemy and avenger to end.

Question: What does the psalmist see when he looks at the sky that causes him to wonder why God pays attention to people?
Response: He sees the heavens, the moon, and the stars that God set in place.

Question: How does the human race compare to the heavenly beings?
Response: Yahweh made humans only a little lower than the heavenly beings.

Question: With what did Yahweh crown humans?
Response: He crowned them with glory and majesty.

Question: What did Yahweh make mankind to do?
Response: Yahweh made mankind to rule over the works of Yahweh’s hands.

Question: What are some things that Yahweh put under mankind’s feet?
Response: He put all sheep and oxen, and even the animals of the field, the birds of the sky, and the fish of the sea, and everything that passes on the paths of the seas under mankind’s feet.

Question: Whose name is magnificent in all the earth?
Response: The name of Yahweh our Lord is magnificent.

Question: How will David give thanks to Yahweh and of what will he tell?
Response: He will give thanks to Yahweh with his whole heart and tell about all his marvelous deeds.

Question: When David’s enemies turn back, what happens to them?
Response: The enemies stumble and perish before Yahweh.

Question: What has the righteous judge done for David?
Response: The righteous judge sits on his throne and has defended David’s just cause.

Question: What does Yahweh do to the nations and the wicked?
Response: He rebukes the nations, destroys the wicked, and blots out their name forever and ever.

Question: What happens to the enemy?
Response: The enemy are finished in desolation forever, and all remembrance of them perishes.

Question: Who sits forever and has established his throne for justice?
Response: Yahweh sits forever and has established his throne for justice.

Question: How does Yahweh judge the world and what does he do for the nations?
Response: Yahweh judges the world fairly and makes just decisions for the nations.

Question: What will Yahweh be for the oppressed?
Response: Yahweh will be a stronghold for the oppressed in times of trouble.

Question: What does the psalmist say about those who know Yahweh’s name?
Response: Those who know Yahweh’s name trust in him, because Yahweh has not abandoned those who seek him.

Question: What does David say the people are to do for Yahweh, who sits in Zion?
Response: They are to sing praises to Yahweh and tell the nations what Yahweh has done.

Question: What does Yahweh not forget?
Response: He does not forget the cry of the poor.

Question: What is David asking Yahweh to do for him?
Response: He is asking Yahweh to have mercy on him.

Question: By whom is David oppressed?
Response: David is oppressed by those who hate him.

Question: Where will David rejoice in salvation?
Response: David will rejoice in salvation in the gates of the daughter of Zion.

Question: What has happened to the nations?
Response: The nations have sunk down into the pit that they made and their feet are caught in the net that they hid.

Question: Who has revealed himself and done justice?
Response: Yahweh has revealed himself and done justice.

Question: Where are the wicked sent?
Response: They are sent to Sheol, along with all the nations that forget God.

Question: What will not happen to the needy and the afflicted?
Response: The needy will not be forgotten forever, nor will the hope of the afflicted perish forever.

Question: What does David want Yahweh to prevent humanity from doing?
Response: David wants Yahweh to arise and not let humanity prevail.

Question: What is David asking Yahweh to do to the nations?
Response: He is asking Yahweh to terrify them and make them know that they are mere men.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh at the beginning of the psalm?
Response: The psalmist asks why Yahweh stands far off and hides in times of trouble.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh to do to the wicked?
Response: The psalmist asks that the wicked be caught in the schemes they have planned to use against the afflicted.

Question: What does the wicked man do regarding Yahweh?
Response: The wicked man despises Yahweh and praises those who steal from others.

Question: Why does the wicked man not seek God?
Response: The wicked man does not seek God because he is so proud.

Question: What does the wicked man say in his heart?
Response: The wicked man says in his heart that he will never be shaken and will never face distress.

Question: What is the wicked man always talking about?
Response: He constantly curses, lies, threatens others, and is always talking about harming others and doing wrong.

Question: What does the wicked man do to innocent people?
Response: He hides in ambush near the villages, murders the innocent in secret, and watches for helpless people.

Question: What animal is the wicked man like?
Response: He is like a lion that hides in a secret place and waits to seize the afflicted.

Question: What does the wicked man say in his heart about God?
Response: The wicked man says that God has forgotten, that God has hidden his face, and that God will never see.

Question: What does the wicked man think about his evil deeds?
Response: He thinks that God will not notice his evil deeds.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh to do?
Response: The psalmist asks Yahweh to take action to prove that he is powerful by paying attention to oppressed people.

Question: What does the wicked man say in his heart?
Response: The wicked man says in his heart that God will not seek him out.

Question: What does the psalmist say Yahweh does for helpless people?
Response: Yahweh sees the mischief done to them, and helpless people and orphans trust him as their helper.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh to do to the wicked?
Response: The psalmist asks Yahweh to make the wicked powerless and their wickedness come to nothing.

Question: What does the psalmist say about Yahweh’s reign?
Response: Yahweh is king forever and ever, and the nations have perished from his land.

Question: How does Yahweh respond to the afflicted?
Response: Yahweh hears the desires of the afflicted, strengthens their heart, attends to them, and acts justly for the orphan and oppressed.

Question: How does Yahweh respond to the needs of the oppressed?
Response: He strengthens their heart, listens to their prayer, and defends them.

Question: In whom does David take refuge?
Response: David takes refuge in Yahweh.

Question: What do some people tell David he should do?
Response: They tell him to flee to the mountains like a bird.

Question: How do the wicked prepare to attack the upright in heart?
Response: They prepare their bows and make ready their arrows while hiding in darkness.

Question: What is Yahweh doing in his holy temple?
Response: His eyes watch and examine all people.

Question: Who does Yahweh test?
Response: He tests both the righteous and the wicked.

Question: Who does Yahweh hate?
Response: He hates those who love to do violence.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh to do to the wicked?
Response: He asks Yahweh to rain snares, fire, and brimstone upon them, with a scorching wind as their portion.

Question: What does Yahweh think of righteousness?
Response: He loves righteousness.

Question: What will happen to the upright?
Response: The upright will see Yahweh’s face.

Question: Why does David ask Yahweh for help?
Response: David asks Yahweh for help because the faithful have come to an end and have vanished from among the sons of man.

Question: How does everyone speak to his neighbor?
Response: Everyone speaks lies to his neighbor and speaks insincerely, with flattering lips.

Question: What does David want Yahweh to do to the flattering lips?
Response: David wants Yahweh to cut off all flattering lips so that they cannot continue to boast.

Question: What do some people boast about?
Response: Some boast that with their tongue they will be powerful, they control what they say, and that no one is master over them.

Question: What will Yahweh do for the poor and the needy?
Response: Yahweh will arise and provide safety for which they long.

Question: What are the words of Yahweh like?
Response: Yahweh’s words are pure words, like silver refined in a furnace and purified seven times.

Question: What does David say Yahweh will do for his people?
Response: David says Yahweh will keep and protect them from this wicked generation forever.

Question: What causes the wicked to walk proudly?
Response: The wicked walk proudly when people praise wicked actions.

Question: What does David repeatedly ask Yahweh at the beginning of this psalm?
Response: David repeatedly asks Yahweh how long he will forget him and hide his face from him.

Question: What is David experiencing while waiting for Yahweh to answer?
Response: David must carry sorrow in his heart every day, and his enemy has the upper hand over him.

Question: How does David ask Yahweh to respond to his prayer?
Response: He asks Yahweh to consider him, answer him, and restore his strength.

Question: What does David fear will happen if Yahweh does not answer?
Response: David fears he will die.

Question: What does David not want his enemy to say about him?
Response: He does not want his enemy to say that he has prevailed over David.

Question: What will happen if David is defeated?
Response: His enemies will rejoice when he is brought down.

Question: In what has David trusted?
Response: David has trusted in Yahweh’s covenant faithfulness.

Question: What does David ask that his heart might do?
Response: He asks that his heart might rejoice in Yahweh’s salvation.

Question: Why will David sing to Yahweh?
Response: David will sing because Yahweh has treated him very generously.

Question: What does a fool say in his heart?
Response: A fool says, “There is no God.”

Question: Why does Yahweh looks down on the sons of mankind?
Response: He looks down to see if there are any who understand, who seek after him.

Question: Who has done evil and corrupted themselves?
Response: Everyone has done evil and corrupt things and no one does what is right.

Question: Who does not call on Yahweh?
Response: Those who make a practice of doing wicked things and acting violently toward Yahweh’s people, do not call on Yahweh.

Question: Why do those who do not call on Yahweh tremble with dread?
Response: They tremble with dread because God is with the righteous assembly.

Question: Who is the refuge of the poor person?
Response: Yahweh is his refuge.

Question: What does David want to come from Zion?
Response: David wants the salvation of Israel to come from Zion.

Question: When will Jacob rejoice and Israel be glad?
Response: When Yahweh restores the prosperity of his people.

Question: What kind of person may stay in Yahweh’s tabernacle and live on his holy hill?
Response: The person who walks blamelessly, does what is right, and speaks truth from his heart may stay in Yahweh’s tabernacle and live on his holy hill.

Question: What does this blameless person not do with his tongue?
Response: He does not insult or say bad things with his tongue.

Question: Who does this blameless person despise?
Response: He despises the person God rejects.

Question: Who does this blameless person honor?
Response: He honors those who fear Yahweh.

Question: When this person swears to his own disadvantage, what does he not do?
Response: He does not take back his promises, even if it causes them trouble to do that.

Question: When this blameless person lends money, what does he not do?
Response: He does not charge interest.

Question: What does this blameless person not take bribes to do?
Response: He does not take bribes to testify against the innocent.

Question: What will never happen to the blameless person who does these things?
Response: He will never be shaken.

Question: What does David ask God to do for him?
Response: David wants God to protect him because he takes refuge in God.

Question: What does David recognize about his relationship to Yahweh?
Response: David recognizes that Yahweh is his Lord, and that everything good David has comes from Yahweh.

Question: Who are the holy ones on the earth?
Response: They are majestic people, and David delights greatly in them.

Question: What will happen to those who hasten after another god?
Response: Their sorrows will be multiplied.

Question: In what ways will David not worship other gods?
Response: He will not pour out drink offerings of blood, and he will not speak their names with his lips.

Question: What does David say Yahweh is to him?
Response: David says Yahweh is the portion of his inheritance and his cup, and Yahweh controls what happens to him.

Question: What does David recognize that Yahweh has done for him?
Response: David recognizes that Yahweh has greatly blessed him.

Question: Why does David bless Yahweh?
Response: David blesses Yahweh because Yahweh counsels him.

Question: How does David’s heart respond to Yahweh?
Response: David’s heart is glad, and he rejoices. He will rest securely.

Question: What does David say Yahweh will not allow to happen to his faithful one?
Response: Yahweh will not abandon his faithful one to Sheol where dead people go and will save him from dying.

Question: What benefits does David get from Yahweh?
Response: Yahweh reveals to David the path of life, gives him joy in Yahweh’s presence, and provides delights at his right hand forever.

Question: What does David ask God to do for him?
Response: David asks Yahweh to listen to his plea for justice, pay attention to his call for help, and give ear to his prayer.

Question: What does David pray will come from Yahweh’s presence?
Response: David prays that his vindication will come from Yahweh’s presence.

Question: What does David say Yahweh found when he tested David at night?
Response: Yahweh examined David’s heart, visited him in the night, and detected nothing wrong; David has determined that he will not sin by what he says.

Question: How has David kept himself from the paths of the violent?
Response: By the word of Yahweh’s lips, David has kept himself from the paths of the violent.

Question: Why does David call on God?
Response: David calls on God because God answers him.

Question: What does David ask God to show him?
Response: He asks God to show his covenant faithfulness to him in a wonderful way.

Question: Who does God save by his right hand?
Response: He saves those who take refuge in him from their enemies.

Question: How does David ask God to protect him from the wicked?
Response: David asks God to protect him as carefully as people protect their own eyes and to protect him from the wicked like mother birds protect their babies under their wings.

Question: How does David describe his enemies?
Response: He says his enemies have no mercy on anyone, and their mouths speak with pride.

Question: What have David’s enemies done to him?
Response: They have surrounded him and set their eyes on him, crouching low to attack him.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to do to his enemies?
Response: He asks Yahweh to arise, confront his enemies and make them bow down, and to rescue him from the wicked with his sword.

Question: From whom is David asking Yahweh to rescue him by his hand?
Response: He is asking Yahweh to rescue him from men of this world whose prosperity is in this life alone.

Question: What does Yahweh provide for the men of this world?
Response: Yahweh fills their bellies with riches; they are satisfied with sons, and they leave their abundance to their children.

Question: What does David expect to happen when he sees Yahweh’s face in righteousness?
Response: He will be satisfied with a sight of Yahweh.

Question: What does David express toward Yahweh?
Response: David says that he loves Yahweh.

Question: What happens when David calls on Yahweh?
Response: David is saved from his enemies.

Question: Where was Yahweh when he heard David’s voice?
Response: Yahweh heard David’s voice from his temple.

Question: Why were the foundations of the mountains shaken?
Response: They were shaken because God was angry.

Question: How did Yahweh come down from the heavens?
Response: With a dark cloud under his feet, he rode on a cherub and flew swiftly on the wings of the wind.

Question: How did Yahweh scatter his enemies?
Response: He shot his lightning like arrows and made his enemies so afraid that they ran away in different directions.

Question: What exposed the foundations of the world?
Response: Yahweh’s rebuke and the blast of the breath of his nostrils exposed the foundations of the world.

Question: Out of what did Yahweh pull David?
Response: It was as if he pulled David out of the deep water of the ocean.

Question: Why did Yahweh save David?
Response: Yahweh saved David because Yahweh delighted in him.

Question: What does Yahweh do to afflicted people?
Response: He saves afflicted people.

Question: What does Yahweh do to those who are proud?
Response: Yahweh humiliates them.

Question: How does David describe God’s way?
Response: He says God’s way is perfect.

Question: What did David do to his enemies?
Response: David pursued his enemies and destroyed them.

Question: What happened when David’s enemies called to Yahweh?
Response: Yahweh did not answer them.

Question: Over what has Yahweh made David the head?
Response: Yahweh made David the head over nations.

Question: From whom does Yahweh deliver David?
Response: Yahweh delivers David from his enemies.

Question: To whom does Yahweh show his covenant faithfulness?
Response: He shows his covenant faithfulness to his anointed one, to David and to his descendants forever.

Question: What do the heavens declare and the sky proclaim?
Response: The heavens declare the glory of God, and the sky proclaims the work of his hands.

Question: What do the days and nights continually declare?
Response: Day after day, words flow forth, and night after night knowledge is declared.

Question: What goes out through all the earth and to the end of the world?
Response: Their words go out through all the earth to the end of the world.

Question: What is the sun like?
Response: The sun is like a bridegroom and like a strong man.

Question: What does the law of Yahweh do?
Response: The law of Yahweh restores the soul.

Question: What does the testimony of Yahweh do?
Response: The testimony of Yahweh makes the simple wise.

Question: What do the precepts of Yahweh do?
Response: The precepts of Yahweh make the heart rejoice.

Question: What does the commandment of Yahweh do?
Response: The commandment of Yahweh shows them how he wants them to behave.

Question: How do the judgments of Yahweh compare to gold?
Response: They are of greater value than gold, even much more than much fine gold.

Question: What do the judgments of Yahweh do for the servant?
Response: By them the servant is warned, and there is great reward in keeping them.

Question: From what does David ask Yahweh to acquit him?
Response: He asks Yahweh to acquit him from his hidden faults.

Question: What does David want to be acceptable to Yahweh?
Response: David wants the words of his mouth and the meditation of his heart to be acceptable to Yahweh.

Question: What does the psalmist pray that Yahweh will do for the king in his time of trouble?
Response: The psalmist prays that Yahweh will answer the king in his time of trouble and protect him.

Question: From where does David want Yahweh to send help and support?
Response: He asks that Yahweh send help from the sanctuary and support from Zion.

Question: What does David want Yahweh to remember and accept?
Response: David asks that Yahweh remember all the grain offerings and accept the burnt offering.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh to grant the king?
Response: The psalmist asks that Yahweh grant the king what his heart desires and fulfill all his plans.

Question: In what does David say they will rejoice?
Response: David says they will rejoice in the salvation that Yahweh gives.

Question: Who does David say that Yahweh will rescue?
Response: David knows that Yahweh will rescue his anointed.

Question: In what do other people trust?
Response: Some trust in chariots and others in horses to defeat their enemies.

Question: What do David and his people do instead of trusting in chariots or horses?
Response: They remember to trust in the power of Yahweh their God.

Question: What does the psalmist say happens to enemies compared to his own people?
Response: The enemies bow down and fall, but the psalmist and his people rise and stand firm.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh to do at the end of the psalm?
Response: The psalmist asks Yahweh to save, and prays that the king will answer them when they call.

Question: In what does the king rejoice?
Response: He rejoices in Yahweh’s strength and in the salvation Yahweh provides.

Question: What has Yahweh done for the king?
Response: Yahweh has given the king his heart’s desire and has not held back the request of his lips.

Question: What has Yahweh brought to the king and placed on his head?
Response: He has brought the king rich blessings and placed a crown of refined gold on his head.

Question: What did Yahweh give the king after the king asked for it?
Response: Yahweh gave the king life and long days forever and ever.

Question: Why is the king’s glory great?
Response: His glory is great because of Yahweh’s salvation.

Question: What has Yahweh bestowed upon the king?
Response: Yahweh has bestowed splendor and majesty.

Question: Why will the king not be shaken?
Response: He will not be shaken because he trusts in Yahweh through the Most High’s covenant faithfulness.

Question: What will Yahweh’s hand find?
Response: Yahweh’s hand will find all his enemies, those who hate him.

Question: What will Yahweh do to his enemies?
Response: He will burn them up, and they will be consumed in his wrath.

Question: What will Yahweh do to the offspring of his enemies?
Response: He will destroy their offspring and their descendants.

Question: Why will the plot of Yahweh’s enemies not succeed?
Response: It will not succeed because Yahweh will turn them back and draw his bow against them.

Question: How will the people exalt Yahweh?
Response: They will sing and praise his power.

Question: What is David asking God?
Response: David is asking why God has abandoned him, why he is so far from David.

Question: What does David do in the daytime and at night?
Response: David cries out in the daytime, and at night David is not silent.

Question: How does David refer to God?
Response: David refers to God as holy, sitting as king with the praises of Israel.

Question: What did David’s ancestors do and how did God respond?
Response: They trusted in God, he rescued them, and they were not disappointed.

Question: How does David describe himself?
Response: David says he is a worm and not a man, a disgrace to humanity and despised by the people.

Question: What do the people that taunt and mock David say to him?
Response: They say that he trusts in Yahweh and that Yahweh should rescue him since Yahweh delights in him.

Question: How long has David trusted God?
Response: David trusted Yahweh when he was on his mother’s breasts and since he was in his mother’s womb.

Question: What is David asking God to do for him?
Response: He is asking God to not be far away from him because trouble is near and there is no one to help.

Question: What is surrounding David like a roaring lion?
Response: His enemies, like many strong bulls of Bashan, surround him, and they are opening their mouths wide against him like a roaring lion.

Question: How does David describe himself?
Response: He says he is being poured out like water, all his bones are dislocated, his heart is like wax, his strength has dried up, his tongue sticks to the roof of his mouth.

Question: What has surrounded David and encircled him?
Response: Dogs have surrounded him and a company of evildoers encircled him, having pierced his hands and feet.

Question: What was done to the garments and the clothes?
Response: The garments were divided and lots were cast for the clothes.

Question: What does David request of Yahweh?
Response: He requests that Yahweh not be far away and hurry to help him.

Question: From what does David want to be rescued and saved?
Response: He wants his life delivered from the sword, rescued from the hand of the dog, and saved from the mouth of the lion and the horns of wild oxen.

Question: What will David declare to his brothers, and where will he praise Yahweh?
Response: He will declare Yahweh’s name to his brothers and praise him in the midst of the assembly.

Question: Who should praise, honor, and stand in awe of Yahweh?
Response: All who fear Yahweh, all descendants of Jacob, and all descendants of Israel should praise, honor, and stand in awe of him.

Question: When did Yahweh hear the afflicted one?
Response: He heard the afflicted one when he cried to him.

Question: Where will David praise and fulfill his vows?
Response: He will praise Yahweh in the great assembly and fulfill his vows before those who fear Yahweh.

Question: Who will eat, be satisfied, and praise Yahweh?
Response: The poor will eat and be satisfied, and those who seek Yahweh will praise him.

Question: What will all the peoples of the earth and what will the families of the nations do?
Response: The peoples of the earth will remember and turn to Yahweh and all the families of the nations will bow down before him.

Question: Over what does Yahweh rule?
Response: Yahweh rules over the nations.

Question: Who will bow before Yahweh?
Response: Both the wealthy of the earth and those who are dying and cannot preserve their own lives will bow before him.

Question: What will future generations do?
Response: They will come and declare to a people not yet born what Yahweh has done.

Question: Who is David’s shepherd?
Response: David’s shepherd is Yahweh.

Question: Where does Yahweh make David lie down?
Response: Yahweh makes David lie down in green pastures.

Question: Where does Yahweh lead David?
Response: Yahweh leads David beside tranquil water.

Question: What does Yahweh restore?
Response: Yahweh restores David’s soul.

Question: Where does Yahweh guide David?
Response: Yahweh guides David along right paths.

Question: When David walks through the valley of deep darkness, who is with him?
Response: Yahweh is with David.

Question: What does Yahweh prepare before David?
Response: Yahweh prepares a table in the presence of David’s enemies.

Question: What does Yahweh anoint?
Response: Yahweh anoints David’s head with oil.

Question: What will pursue David all the days of his life?
Response: Goodness and covenant faithfulness will pursue David.

Question: Where will David live all the days of his life?
Response: David will live in the house of Yahweh for a very long time.

Question: What does the psalmist say belongs to Yahweh?
Response: The earth and its fullness belong to Yahweh, along with the world and everyone who lives in it, because he founded it upon the seas and established it upon the rivers.

Question: Who will ascend the mountain of Yahweh and stand in his holy place?
Response: Those who have clean hands and a pure heart, who have not lifted up their soul to what is false and have not sworn deceitfully, will ascend Yahweh’s mountain and stand in his holy place.

Question: What will those who seek Yahweh receive?
Response: Those who seek Yahweh and seek his face will receive a blessing and righteousness from the God of their salvation.

Question: Why are the gates to be lifted up?
Response: So that the King of glory may come in.

Question: Who is the King of glory?
Response: Yahweh is the King of glory. He is strong and mighty in battle, and he commands armies.

Question: For what does David ask God?
Response: David asks God to not let him be shamed, and to not let his enemies rejoice over him.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to teach him?
Response: David asks Yahweh to make known to him Yahweh’s ways and paths, and to lead him in Yahweh’s truth.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to remember?
Response: David asks Yahweh to remember his compassion and covenant faithfulness, but not to remember the sins of David’s youth or his transgressions.

Question: What does Yahweh do because he is good and upright?
Response: He teaches the sinful in the way, leads the humble in justice, and teaches the humble his way.

Question: What are all the paths of Yahweh?
Response: They are covenant love and faithfulness for those who keep his covenant and his testimonies.

Question: Why does David ask Yahweh to forgive his iniquity?
Response: David asks Yahweh to forgive his iniquity for the sake of Yahweh’s name.

Question: What will Yahweh do for the man who fears Yahweh?
Response: Yahweh will teach him in the way he is to choose, his life will remain in prosperity, and his descendants will inherit the land.

Question: What does Yahweh share with those who fear him?
Response: Yahweh shares his secret with those who fear him and makes his covenant known to them.

Question: Why does David continually keep his eyes on Yahweh?
Response: Because Yahweh will rescue David when his enemies attempt to hurt him.

Question: When David is alone and afflicted, what does he request of Yahweh?
Response: David asks Yahweh to turn toward him and be gracious to him.

Question: When the troubles of David’s heart are enlarged, what does he ask of Yahweh?
Response: David asks Yahweh to bring him out of his distresses, to see his affliction and his trouble and forgive all of his sins, and to see his enemies who are many and who hate him with violent hatred.

Question: Why does David ask Yahweh to not let him be shamed?
Response: Because David takes refuge in Yahweh.

Question: From what does David ask God to redeem Israel?
Response: He asks God to redeem Israel from all of his troubles.

Question: How does David say that he has walked?
Response: David says he has walked with integrity and has trusted in Yahweh without wavering.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to examine and test?
Response: He asks Yahweh to test the purity of his inner parts and heart.

Question: What is before David’s eyes?
Response: Yahweh’s covenant faithfulness is before David’s eyes.

Question: With whom does David not associate or mingle?
Response: David does not associate with deceitful people or mingle with dishonest people.

Question: What does David hate?
Response: David hates gathering with evil people.

Question: With whom does David say he will not sit?
Response: David says he will not sit with the wicked.

Question: What does David do as he goes around Yahweh’s altar?
Response: He sings songs of thanksgiving and recounts all of Yahweh’s wonderful acts.

Question: How does David feel about the place where Yahweh lives?
Response: He loves the place where Yahweh lives and his glory appears.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh not to do?
Response: David asks Yahweh not to gather his soul with the sinful or his life with men of blood.

Question: How does David say that he will walk?
Response: David says that he will walk in integrity.

Question: Where does David’s foot stand and where will he bless Yahweh?
Response: His foot stands on level ground and he will bless Yahweh among the assemblies.

Question: Why should David not fear anyone?
Response: David should not fear anyone since Yahweh is his light, his salvation, and the refuge of his life.

Question: What happened when evildoers advanced against David?
Response: His enemies and those who were hostile to him stumbled and fell.

Question: In what situations will David not fear but rather remain confident?
Response: His heart will not fear even though an army encamps against him and war rises up against him.

Question: What has David asked of Yahweh?
Response: David has asked that he may dwell in the house of Yahweh all the days of his life, to look upon the beauty of Yahweh, and to inquire in his temple.

Question: What will Yahweh do for David in the day of trouble?
Response: Yahweh will protect him in his tabernacle and keep him safe as if he were setting him high upon a rock where David’s enemies cannot reach him.

Question: How will David offer worship to Yahweh?
Response: David will offer sacrifices of joy, sing, and make music to Yahweh.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to do?
Response: David asks Yahweh to hear his voice, to be gracious to him, and to answer him.

Question: What does David’s heart say to Yahweh?
Response: David’s heart says that he will seek Yahweh’s face.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh not to do?
Response: David asks Yahweh not to hide his face from him, not to turn his servant away in anger, nor to abandon or leave him.

Question: What does David say Yahweh will do even though his father and mother have left him?
Response: David says that Yahweh will care for him even though his father and mother have left him.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to do because of his enemies?
Response: David asks Yahweh to teach him Yahweh’s way and guide him so that he remains safe from his enemies.

Question: Why does David ask Yahweh not to give him up to his adversaries?
Response: David asks this because false witnesses and violent accusers have risen against him.

Question: What did David believe that helped him?
Response: David believed that he would see the goodness of Yahweh and trust him as long as he lived.

Question: What does David encourage the people to do?
Response: David encourages the people to wait on Yahweh, to be strong, and to let their heart be courageous.

Question: To whom does David cry out?
Response: David cries out to Yahweh, his rock.

Question: What will happen to David if Yahweh does not respond to him?
Response: David will join those who go down to the grave.

Question: Who does David want to hear the sound of his pleading?
Response: David wants Yahweh to hear the sound of his pleading.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh not to do?
Response: David asks Yahweh not to drag him away with the wicked.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to give the wicked?
Response: David asks Yahweh to give them what their deeds deserve and to repay them for their evil deeds.

Question: What do the wicked not give heed to?
Response: The wicked do not give heed to the works of Yahweh or the action of his hands.

Question: What has Yahweh heard?
Response: Yahweh has heard the sound of David’s pleading.

Question: Who is David’s strength and shield?
Response: Yahweh is David’s strength and shield.

Question: In whom does David’s heart trust?
Response: David’s heart trusts in Yahweh and greatly rejoices.

Question: Who is the strength of his people?
Response: Yahweh is the strength of his people.

Question: Who is the saving refuge of his anointed one?
Response: Yahweh is the saving refuge of his anointed one.

Question: Who does David want Yahweh to save and bless?
Response: David wants Yahweh to save his people and bless his inheritance.

Question: Who does David say should ascribe glory and strength to Yahweh?
Response: David says the sons of God should ascribe glory and strength to Yahweh.

Question: To what does David compare the voice of Yahweh?
Response: David compares the voice of Yahweh to thunder over the waters.

Question: What does Yahweh break?
Response: Yahweh breaks the cedars of Lebanon.

Question: What does Yahweh shake?
Response: Yahweh shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.

Question: What do the people in Yahweh’s temple declare?
Response: Everyone in his temple declares Yahweh’s glory.

Question: How long does Yahweh sit as king?
Response: Yahweh sits as king forever.

Question: What two things will Yahweh give his people?
Response: Yahweh will give strength to his people and will bless his people with peace.

Question: Why does David extol Yahweh?
Response: He extols Yahweh because Yahweh has drawn him up and has not allowed David’s enemies to rejoice over him.

Question: What did Yahweh do when David cried for help?
Response: Yahweh healed David.

Question: In what way did Yahweh restore David?
Response: Yahweh caused David to get well and restored him when he was about to die.

Question: What are the faithful told to do in remembrance of Yahweh’s holiness?
Response: The faithful are told to sing praise to Yahweh and to give him thanks.

Question: How long does Yahweh’s anger and favor last?
Response: Yahweh’s anger is only for a moment, but his favor is for a lifetime.

Question: What happens after weeping comes for a night?
Response: Joy comes in the morning.

Question: What did David say when his life was going well?
Response: David said that he would never be shaken.

Question: What happened to David when Yahweh hid his face?
Response: When Yahweh hid his face, David was dismayed.

Question: What did David seek from the Lord?
Response: David sought favor from the Lord.

Question: What things is David asking Yahweh to do for him?
Response: David is asking Yahweh to hear him, to be gracious to him, and to be his helper.

Question: What has Yahweh done for David?
Response: Yahweh has turned David’s mourning into dancing, causing him to change from being sad to being joyful.

Question: What does David say his glory will do?
Response: David’s glory will sing praise to Yahweh and not be silent. He will give thanks to Yahweh his God forever.

Question: In whom does David take refuge?
Response: David takes refuge in Yahweh.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to become for him?
Response: He asks Yahweh to be his rocky summit of refuge and a stronghold to save him.

Question: Why does Yahweh lead and guide David?
Response: Yahweh leads and guides David for the sake of his own name.

Question: What does David believe that Yahweh will protect him from?
Response: Yahweh will rescue him from the evil plans his enemies have made to hurt him.

Question: Into what does David commit his spirit?
Response: David puts himself into the care of Yahweh.

Question: Who does David hate?
Response: He hates those who keep idols of worthlessness.

Question: Why will David be glad and rejoice in Yahweh’s covenant faithfulness?
Response: David will be glad and rejoice because Yahweh has seen his affliction and known the troubles of his soul.

Question: Where has Yahweh set David’s feet?
Response: Yahweh has set David’s feet in an open space.

Question: Why does David ask Yahweh to be gracious to him?
Response: David is in distress and his eye has wasted away from grief.

Question: Why does David’s strength fail and his bones waste away?
Response: His strength fails and his bones waste away because of his iniquity.

Question: How do people respond to David?
Response: His enemies treat him with contempt, his neighbors are especially horrified by him, and those who see him outside flee from him.

Question: To what does David compare himself?
Response: David says he is as forgotten as a dead man whom no one thinks about, and that he is like a broken vessel.

Question: Why is David so troubled?
Response: He hears the whispering of many and there is terror on every side, as his enemies conspire together to take his life.

Question: From whom does David ask God to rescue him?
Response: He asks God to rescue him from his enemies and from those who pursue him.

Question: What does David ask God to do for his servant?
Response: David asks God to make his face shine upon his servant and to save him in Yahweh’s covenant faithfulness.

Question: What does David ask God not to let happen to him?
Response: David asks Yahweh not to let him have shame.

Question: Why should lying lips be silenced?
Response: They speak arrogant and contemptuous things against the righteous.

Question: For whom is God’s goodness stored up?
Response: Yahweh’s goodness is stored up for those who fear him.

Question: From what does Yahweh protect those who revere him?
Response: He protects them from the evil plots of the enemies who accuse them.

Question: Why is Yahweh to be blessed?
Response: Yahweh showed David his marvelous covenant faithfulness when David was in a besieged city.

Question: What did Yahweh do for David even though David said that he was cut off from God’s eyes?
Response: Yahweh heard his plea for help when David cried out to him.

Question: What does Yahweh do for his faithful followers?
Response: Yahweh preserves the faithful but pays back the proud in full.

Question: What advice does David give to those who wait for Yahweh?
Response: He tells them to be firm and courageous.

Question: Who does the psalmist describe as truly happy?
Response: The psalmist describes as truly happy those whose transgression is lifted up and whose sin is covered.

Question: What happened to David when he did not confess his sins?
Response: His bones wasted away and he groaned all day long. Yahweh’s hand was heavy on him day and night, and his vitality was sapped by the summer heat.

Question: What did God do when David acknowledged his sin and no longer hid his iniquity?
Response: God forgave the guilt of David’s sin.

Question: What should godly people do when they realize they have sinned?
Response: They should pray to Yahweh when they realize that they have sinned.

Question: What assurance does the psalmist give to the godly who pray to Yahweh?
Response: A flood of great waters will not reach them.

Question: With what does Yahweh surround the psalmist?
Response: Yahweh surrounds him with shouts of deliverance.

Question: What does Yahweh promise to do for those who come to him?
Response: Yahweh promises to instruct and teach them about the way they should go, and to counsel them personally.

Question: What does the psalmist warn against being like?
Response: The psalmist warns against being like a horse or mule without understanding, which must be controlled with a bit and bridle.

Question: What will happen to the one who trusts in Yahweh?
Response: Yahweh’s covenant faithfulness will surround him.

Question: What does the psalmist call on the righteous and upright in heart to do?
Response: The psalmist calls them to be glad in Yahweh, rejoice, and shout for joy.

Question: In whom does the psalmist say the righteous should rejoice?
Response: The righteous should rejoice in Yahweh.

Question: With what instruments does the psalmist say to play to give thanks to Yahweh?
Response: The psalmist says to give thanks to Yahweh with the harp and with the lyre of ten strings.

Question: How does the psalmist describe Yahweh’s word and work?
Response: Yahweh’s word is upright and all his work is done in faithfulness.

Question: What does Yahweh love?
Response: Yahweh loves righteousness and justice.

Question: What was made by the word of Yahweh?
Response: The heavens and the stars were created by Yahweh commanding it.

Question: What does the psalmist say Yahweh does to the waters of the sea?
Response: He gathers the waters of the sea together like a heap.

Question: What does the psalmist say all the inhabitants of the world should do?
Response: All the inhabitants of the world should deeply revere Yahweh.

Question: How long will the counsel of Yahweh stand?
Response: The counsel of Yahweh stands forever, the plans of his heart to every generation.

Question: On whom does Yahweh look down from where he lives?
Response: Yahweh looks down on all the people, on all who live on the earth.

Question: Whom does Yahweh watch over?
Response: Yahweh watches over those who deeply revere him and confidently expect him to faithfully love them.

Question: Why does the psalmist say the heart rejoices in Yahweh?
Response: The heart rejoices in Yahweh because the people trust in his holy name.

Question: How often will David praise Yahweh?
Response: David will praise Yahweh at all times.

Question: Who does David want to hear when he boasts in Yahweh?
Response: David wants the humble to hear it and rejoice.

Question: What is David asking people to do with him?
Response: David is asking them to magnify Yahweh and exalt his name together.

Question: What happened when David sought Yahweh?
Response: Yahweh answered David and delivered him from all his fears.

Question: What happens to those who look to Yahweh?
Response: They are radiant and their faces are not ashamed.

Question: What did Yahweh do when this afflicted man called out to him?
Response: Yahweh heard him and saved him from all his troubles.

Question: What does the angel of Yahweh do when he encamps around those who fear him?
Response: The angel of Yahweh rescues them.

Question: Who is the man who is happy?
Response: The man who takes refuge in Yahweh is happy.

Question: What does the psalmist say is true for those who fear Yahweh?
Response: There is no lack for those who fear Yahweh.

Question: What does David say about those who seek Yahweh?
Response: Those who seek Yahweh will not lack anything good.

Question: What does David invite the sons to do?
Response: David invites them to come and listen to him so that he can teach them the fear of Yahweh.

Question: What does the psalmist say a man who desires life should do?
Response: He should guard his tongue from evil and his lips from speaking deceit, turn away from evil, do good, seek peace, and pursue it.

Question: How does Yahweh pay attention to the righteous?
Response: Yahweh pays attention and listens when the righteous call to him for help.

Question: What will Yahweh do to the doers of evil?
Response: Yahweh will cut off their remembrance from the earth.

Question: What does Yahweh do when the righteous cry out?
Response: He hears and delivers them from all their troubles.

Question: To whom is Yahweh near?
Response: He is near the brokenhearted.

Question: Who does Yahweh save?
Response: Yahweh saves those with a crushed spirit.

Question: What happens to the righteous who have many troubles?
Response: Yahweh delivers him from all of them and guards all his bones.

Question: What will happen to the wicked?
Response: Evil will kill the wicked.

Question: What will happen to those who hate the righteous?
Response: Those who hate the righteous will be found guilty.

Question: What does Yahweh do for his servants?
Response: He redeems the soul of his servants, and those who take refuge in him will not be guilty.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to do for him?
Response: David asks Yahweh to work against those who work against him, and fight against those who fight against him.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to do?
Response: He asks Yahweh to protect him and come to save him.

Question: What does David want to happen to those who plan to harm him?
Response: David wants them to be turned back and confounded.

Question: Who does David want to drive away his enemies?
Response: He wants the angel of Yahweh to drive them away.

Question: What did David’s enemies do without cause?
Response: They tried to kill David as though they had set their net and dug a pit for him to fall into.

Question: What does David want to happen to his enemies?
Response: David wants destruction to overtake them by surprise, for them to be caught in their own nets, and for them to fall to their destruction.

Question: In what will David rejoice?
Response: He will rejoice in Yahweh’s salvation.

Question: Why is David sorrowful?
Response: He is sorrowful because unrighteous witnesses rise up to accuse him falsely, and repay him evil for good.

Question: How did David respond to his enemies’ troubles?
Response: David wore sackcloth, fasted for them, went about in grief and bent down in mourning for them.

Question: How did his enemies respond to David’s suffering?
Response: They rejoiced and celebrated and then gathered to attack, slander, and mock him in anger.

Question: When will David thank Yahweh in front of his people?
Response: He will thank Yahweh when he is rescued from his enemies’ destructive attacks.

Question: What do David’s enemies do instead of speaking peace?
Response: They devise deceitful words against those who live in peace.

Question: What does David want Yahweh to do since he has seen David’s troubles?
Response: He wants Yahweh to not be silent, to not be far away, and to arouse and awake to his defense.

Question: Why does David say Yahweh should defend him?
Response: He says Yahweh should defend him because of Yahweh’s righteousness.

Question: How does the wicked man respond to his own sin?
Response: They are so proud that they refuse to recognize how wrongly they act.

Question: What are the wicked man’s words like?
Response: His words are sinful and deceitful.

Question: What does the wicked man not want to be?
Response: He does not want to be wise and to do good.

Question: What does the wicked man do while he lies in bed?
Response: He plans ways to sin while he lies in bed.

Question: What reaches to the heavens and to the clouds?
Response: Yahweh’s covenant faithfulness reaches to the heavens, and his trustworthiness reaches to the clouds.

Question: What is Yahweh’s righteousness like?
Response: His righteousness is as permanent as the mountains of God, and his judgments are vast and mysterious as the deepest oceans.

Question: What does Yahweh take care of?
Response: Yahweh takes care of all living creatures, both people and animals.

Question: To what does the psalmist compare how God protects people?
Response: God protects people just as a bird shelters its young under its wings.

Question: Who is the fountain of life by whose light we see?
Response: Yahweh is the one who causes everything to live and makes it possible to understand what is true.

Question: To whom does David ask God to extend his covenant faithfulness?
Response: David asks God to extend God’s covenant faithfulness fully to those who know God.

Question: What does David ask God not to let happen?
Response: David asks God not to let the foot of the arrogant man come near him, and the hand of the wicked to drive him away.

Question: What has happened to the evildoers?
Response: They have fallen and are knocked down and are not able to get up.

Question: Why does David say to not be upset because of evildoers?
Response: He says to not be worried about evildoers because they will soon dry up as the grass and plants wither and die.

Question: Why does David say to delight in Yahweh?
Response: He says to delight in Yahweh and he will give you the desires of your heart.

Question: Why does David say to trust in Yahweh?
Response: He says to trust in Yahweh and he will act on your behalf, display your justice like the daylight and your innocence like the day at noon.

Question: What does David say people should do before Yahweh?
Response: David says to be still before Yahweh and wait patiently for him, and to not be angry if someone succeeds with their evil plans.

Question: Why does David say to not be angry and frustrated or to worry about evildoers?
Response: They will be cut off and disappear, but those who wait for Yahweh will inherit the land.

Question: What will happen to the meek?
Response: They will inherit the land and delight in peacefulness.

Question: What will happen to the wicked who have tried to kill those who are upright?
Response: The swords of the wicked will pierce their own hearts and their bows will be broken.

Question: Why is the little that the righteous have better than the abundance of many wicked people?
Response: The little that the righteous have is better because the arms of the wicked people will be broken, but Yahweh will support the righteous people.

Question: How will Yahweh provide for the blameless?
Response: The blameless will not be ashamed when times are bad, they will have enough to eat, and their heritage will be forever.

Question: What will happen to Yahweh’s enemies?
Response: His enemies will be like the glory of the pastures which will be consumed and disappear in the smoke.

Question: What is the benefit of having Yahweh establish a man’s steps?
Response: Even if something bad happens to him, he will not fail completely for Yahweh is taking care of him.

Question: What has David not seen happen to the righteous person?
Response: He has not seen the righteous person abandoned or his children begging for bread.

Question: How does the righteous person act?
Response: He is gracious, generous, and he lends freely.

Question: What should people do to live in the land forever?
Response: They should turn away from evil and do what is right.

Question: What will the righteous inherit?
Response: They will inherit the land and live there forever.

Question: What does the mouth of the righteous person speak?
Response: The mouth of the righteous person speaks wisdom and increases justice.

Question: What will happen to the righteous person whom the wicked person seeks to kill?
Response: Yahweh will not abandon him into the evil person’s hand nor condemn him when he is judged.

Question: What will happen to those who wait for Yahweh?
Response: Yahweh will raise them up to possess the land.

Question: What will happen to the wicked and violent person?
Response: He had been powerful but then he passed away and was nowhere to be found.

Question: What does Yahweh do for the righteous?
Response: Yahweh protects them in time of trouble, and helps and rescues them.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh not to do?
Response: He asks Yahweh not to rebuke him in his anger and not to punish him in his wrath.

Question: What does the psalmist say Yahweh’s discipline feels like to him?
Response: He says he feels as though Yahweh had shot arrows into him and his hand had struck him.

Question: Why does the psalmist say his whole body is sick?
Response: His whole body is sick because of Yahweh’s anger, and there is no peace in his bones because of his sin.

Question: How does the psalmist describe his iniquities?
Response: He says his iniquities overwhelm him and are a burden too heavy for him to bear.

Question: What has happened to the psalmist’s wounds?
Response: His wounds are infected and smell because of his foolish sins.

Question: How does the psalmist describe his daily condition?
Response: He is stooped over and humiliated every day, and he goes about mourning all day long.

Question: What fills the psalmist within?
Response: He is filled with burning from within, and there is no health in his flesh.

Question: How does the psalmist describe the extent of his suffering?
Response: He is numb and utterly crushed, and he groans because of the anguish of his heart.

Question: What does the psalmist say the Lord knows about him?
Response: The Lord understands his heart’s deepest yearnings, and his groanings are not hidden from the Lord.

Question: What three things does the psalmist say are happening to him physically?
Response: His heart is pounding, his strength is fading, and his eyesight is dimming.

Question: How do the psalmist’s friends and neighbors respond to his condition?
Response: His friends and loved ones shun him because of his condition, and his relatives stand far away.

Question: What do those who seek the psalmist’s life do to him?
Response: They lay snares for him and speak destructive and deceitful words against him all day long.

Question: How does the psalmist respond to those who seek to harm him?
Response: He acts like a deaf man who hears nothing and a mute man who says nothing in reply.

Question: In whom does the psalmist place his hope?
Response: He waits for Yahweh and trusts that the Lord his God will answer him.

Question: What does the psalmist fear will happen if his foot slips?
Response: He fears his enemies will rejoice and gloat over him.

Question: What two things does the psalmist say about his current situation?
Response: He says he is about to stumble and that he is in constant pain.

Question: What does the psalmist confess to Yahweh?
Response: He confesses his guilt and says he is troubled about his sin.

Question: How do the psalmist’s enemies treat him?
Response: His enemies are numerous, hate him without cause, repay him evil for good, and accuse him even though he has pursued what is good.

Question: How do his enemies repay the good he has done?
Response: They repay David evil for good and they hurl accusations at him.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh not to do?
Response: He asks Yahweh not to abandon him and not to stay far away from him.

Question: What names does David use when he asks Yahweh for help?
Response: He calls him, “Yahweh”, “God”, “Lord”, and “my salvation.”

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh to do?
Response: He asks Yahweh to come quickly to help him.

Question: What did David decide to do?
Response: David decided to watch what he said so that he would not sin with his tongue. He would keep silent while in the presence of an evil man.

Question: What happened when David kept silent and kept back his words from saying anything good?
Response: David’s pain grew worse, and his heart became hot and burned like a fire when he thought about what was happening.

Question: What does David want to know about his life?
Response: David wants to know when his life will end, how long his days are, and how transient he is.

Question: What does David say his life is like before Yahweh?
Response: David’s lifetime is like nothing before Yahweh.

Question: What does David say everyone hurries to do?
Response: David says everyone hurries to accumulate riches, even though they do not know who will receive them after they die.

Question: Who is David’s only hope?
Response: The Lord is David’s only hope.

Question: What does David ask the Lord to rescue him from?
Response: David asks the Lord to rescue him from his sins.

Question: Of what does David not want to be the object?
Response: David does not want to be the object of the ridicule of foolish people.

Question: How does David respond to what the Lord did?
Response: David is silent and does not open his mouth.

Question: What does David ask the Lord to stop?
Response: David asks the Lord to stop punishing him as he feels he is about to die.

Question: What happens when the Lord disciplines people for sin?
Response: When the Lord disciplines people for sin, he destroys what they treasure, and only gives them a short time to live.

Question: Why does David ask Yahweh to hear his prayer?
Response: David asks because he is living before Yahweh on earth only for a short time, a refugee like all his ancestors were.

Question: What does David ask Yahweh to do before David dies?
Response: David asks Yahweh to leave him alone so that David may be happy again before he dies.

Question: What did Yahweh do after David waited patiently for him?
Response: Yahweh listened to David and heard his cry.

Question: What did Yahweh do for David?
Response: Yahweh rescued David from the terrible things that had been happening to him as if he’d been in a pit of mud but was lifted out onto a safe and solid rock.

Question: What will many do because God has put a new song in David’s mouth?
Response: Many will see it and honor him and will trust in Yahweh.

Question: Who is blessed?
Response: The man is blessed who makes Yahweh his trust and does not honor the proud or those who turn away from Yahweh to lies.

Question: How does David describe God’s deeds and thoughts?
Response: God’s wonderful deeds are many and his thoughts about us cannot be numbered.

Question: What is written about David in the scroll of God’s law?
Response: It is written that he delights to do God’s will and that he deeply treasures God’s laws.

Question: What has David proclaimed in the great assembly?
Response: He has proclaimed good news of God’s righteousness in the great assembly.

Question: What has David not concealed?
Response: He has not concealed God’s righteousness in his heart nor God’s covenant faithfulness nor trustworthiness from the great assembly.

Question: What does David pray that Yahweh will do for him?
Response: David prays that Yahweh not keep back his acts of mercy from David, but rather, let Yahweh’s covenant faithfulness and his trustworthiness always preserve David.

Question: What does David say is happening to him?
Response: Troubles that cannot be numbered surround him, and his iniquities have caught up with him so that he can no longer see clearly. They are more than the hairs on his head, and his heart has failed him.

Question: What does David pray that Yahweh will be pleased to do?
Response: He prays that Yahweh will be pleased to rescue him.

Question: What does David pray will happen to those who pursue his life?
Response: He prays that they have shame and be completely disappointed, and that they be turned back and brought to dishonor.

Question: What does David pray for those who seek Yahweh and love his salvation?
Response: He prays that they rejoice and be glad in Yahweh, and that they say continually, “May Yahweh be great.”

Question: How does David describe the Lord?
Response: The Lord thinks about him, is his help, and comes to David’s rescue.

Question: Who does David say is the one who is blessed?
Response: The one who is helps powerless people has a good life.

Question: What will Yahweh do for the person who considers the lowly?
Response: Yahweh will rescue him, preserve him and keep him alive, and he will be happy on the earth.

Question: What will Yahweh not do to the blessed person?
Response: Yahweh will not turn him over to the will of his enemies.

Question: What will Yahweh do for the blessed person on the bed of suffering?
Response: Yahweh will support him on the bed of suffering and make his bed of sickness into a bed of healing.

Question: Why did David ask Yahweh to have mercy on him?
Response: He asked Yahweh to be gracious to him because he had sinned against Yahweh.

Question: What evil do his enemies speak against David?
Response: They ask when he will die and his name will perish.

Question: What does David’s enemy say to him?
Response: His enemy says worthless things.

Question: What do those who hate David whisper and hope for?
Response: Those who hate David whisper together against him and plan for his hurt.

Question: What has David’s own close friend in whom he trusted done?
Response: His own close friend has betrayed him.

Question: What does the psalmist ask Yahweh to do for him?
Response: The psalmist asks Yahweh to have mercy on him and raise him up so that he can pay back his enemies.

Question: How does David know that Yahweh delights in him?
Response: David knows this because his enemy does not triumph over him.

Question: How does Yahweh help David?
Response: Yahweh upholds him in his integrity and will keep David before his face forever.

Question: When may Yahweh be praised?
Response: Yahweh may be praised from always, from everlasting to everlasting.

Question: How does the psalmist describe his longing for God?
Response: He says his soul longs for God just as a deer pants for streams of water.

Question: What does the psalmist say his soul does?
Response: His soul thirsts for God, for the living God.

Question: What has the psalmist’s crying become for him?
Response: His weeping has taken the place of his food day and night.

Question: What does the psalmist call to mind?
Response: He calls to mind how he went with the crowd and led them in procession to the house of God with a voice of rejoicing and praise.

Question: What does the psalmist tell his soul to do?
Response: He tells his soul to wait for God, for he will again thank God, the one who brings him salvation.

Question: What do the psalmists say passes over them?
Response: He says all of God’s breakers and waves pass over him.

Question: What does Yahweh command in the daytime?
Response: Yahweh commands his covenant faithfulness in the daytime.

Question: What happens at night for the psalmist?
Response: At night, God’s song is with the psalmist, a prayer to the God of his life.

Question: What does the psalmist say to God, his rock?
Response: He asks why God has forgotten him, and why David walks about mourning because of the oppression of the enemy.

Question: What do the psalmist’s enemies say to him, and how does it affect him?
Response: They mock him all day by asking where his God is, and it feels like crushing pain in his bones.

Question: What does the psalmist tell his soul at the end of the psalm?
Response: He tells his soul to wait for God, for he will again praise God, who saves him.

Question: What does the psalmist ask God to do for him?
Response: He asks God to vindicate him and plead his case against a nation that is not faithful.

Question: Why does the psalmist appeal to God to vindicate him?
Response: He appeals to God because God is the God of his refuge.

Question: What does the psalmist ask God’s light and faithfulness to do?
Response: He asks them to lead him and bring him to the mountain of God’s holiness and to God’s dwellings.

Question: Where does the psalmist want to come when God brings him there?
Response: He wants to come to the altar of God.

Question: What will the psalmist do with the lyre?
Response: He will thank God with the lyre.

Question: What does the psalmist tell his soul to do instead of being cast down?
Response: He tells his soul to wait for God, for he will again thank God who is his salvation and his God.

Question: What have the psalmists heard with their ears?
Response: They have heard what work God did in their days, in the days of old.

Question: What did God do with his hand?
Response: God drove out the nations with his hand and planted the people of Israel.

Question: How did the Israelites obtain the land?
Response: They did not obtain the land by their own sword or by their own arm, but by God’s right hand because he favored them.

Question: What do the Israelites do through God?
Response: Through God they defeat their enemies, and through God’s name they trample those who rise up against them.

Question: In what does the psalmist not trust?
Response: He does not trust in his bow, and his sword does not save him.

Question: From what does God save them?
Response: God saves them from their enemies and shames those who hate them.

Question: What has God done?
Response: God has rejected and humiliated them and no longer goes out with their armies.

Question: What does God make the Israelites do?
Response: God makes them turn back from the adversary.

Question: What do those who hate the Israelites do?
Response: Those who hate them take spoil for themselves.

Question: What has God made the Israelites like?
Response: God has made them like sheep destined for slaughter and has scattered them as slaves among the nations.

Question: What has God made the Israelites to their neighbors?
Response: God has made them a reproach to their neighbors, a scorn and a derision to those around them.

Question: Why is the psalmist’s dishonor before him all the day long?
Response: His dishonor is before him, and the shame of his face has covered him because of the voice of him who rebukes and insults, because of the enemy and the avenger.

Question: What have the Israelites not done?
Response: They have not forgotten God nor dealt falsely with God’s covenant.

Question: What have the Israelites’ heart and steps not done?
Response: Their heart has not turned back, nor have their steps turned aside from God’s way.

Question: What has God done to them?
Response: God has allowed their enemies to ruin their land as if darkness has covered it.

Question: What would God discover?
Response: God would discover whether they had forgotten the name of their God or spread out their hands to a foreign god.

Question: What are the Israelites considered to be because of God?
Response: They are considered to be like sheep for slaughter.

Question: What has happened to the Israelites?
Response: They are helpless as if they had fallen down and could not get up.

Question: What does the psalmist pray that God would do?
Response: He prays that God might rise up for their help and redeem them for the sake of God’s covenant faithfulness.

Question: What is the psalmist reciting to the king?
Response: He is reciting his works to the king.

Question: How does the psalmist describe the king?
Response: The king is fairer than the sons of man, grace is poured onto his lips, and God has blessed him forever.

Question: Why should the king ride out?
Response: The king should ride out for the sake of truth, humility, and righteousness.

Question: How are the king’s arrows described?
Response: His arrows are sharp as if piercing the hearts of the king’s enemies.

Question: How is God’s throne and a scepter of justice described?
Response: His throne is forever and ever, and a scepter of justice is the scepter of God’s kingdom.

Question: What has the king loved and hated?
Response: He loves righteousness and hates wickedness.

Question: Of what do all of the king’s garments smell?
Response: All of his garments smell of myrrh, aloes, and cassia.

Question: Where are the daughters of kings and the queen?
Response: Daughters of kings are among the king’s precious ones, and the queen stands at his right hand adorned in gold of Ophir.

Question: What will the rich do?
Response: The rich among the people will beg for a favor.

Question: Where will the royal daughter be led?
Response: She will be led to the king in embroidered dress.

Question: How and where will the virgins be led?
Response: They will be led by joy and rejoicing; they will enter into the palace of the king.

Question: Who will take the place of the king’s fathers?
Response: His sons will take the place of his fathers, and he will make them princes throughout all the earth.

Question: What does the psalmist want the peoples of all generations to do regarding the king?
